Ayushman Bharat PMJAY Eligibility Criteria 2026

Find out if you qualify for India's largest government-funded healthcare program providing Rs 5 lakh annual cashless health cover per family.

Fact-checked and verified for 2026

What is the Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY Scheme?

Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY) is the flagship national health insurance scheme of the Government of India. It provides cashless and paperless secondary and tertiary healthcare treatment for poor, vulnerable, and deprived families.

Eligible families get a health cover of Rs 5 lakh per family per year for treatment at empaneled government and private hospitals across India. There is no limit on family size, age, or gender under the scheme.

Quick checker tool available

Skip the long reading if you are in a hurry and use our step-by-step evaluator to check eligibility online.

Start Eligibility Check

Rural Eligibility Criteria

In rural areas, eligibility is primarily based on housing, landownership, and deprivation categories recorded in the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC 2011). Families falling under any of the following deprivation categories are eligible:

Deprivation codeDeprivation category descriptionEligibility status
D1Only one room with kucha walls and kucha roof.Eligible
D2No adult member between the ages of 16 and 59 inside the household.Eligible
D3Female-headed households with no adult male member between 16 and 59.Eligible
D4Disabled member inside the household and no able-bodied adult member.Eligible
D5Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe rural households.Eligible
D7Landless households deriving a major part of their income from manual casual labor.Eligible

Additionally, rural families living in destitution, manual scavengers, primitive tribal groups, and legally released bonded laborers are treated as eligible without needing deprivation checks.

Urban Eligibility: Approved Occupational Categories

In urban areas, eligibility is based on the specific occupation of household members. The scheme lists broad informal and casual worker categories that qualify:

  • Ragpickers and street sweepers
  • Beggars and those surviving on alms
  • Domestic workers and home-based helpers
  • Street vendors, hawkers, cobblers, and vegetable sellers
  • Construction workers, plumbers, masons, painters, and welders
  • Security guards, coolies, and other loaders
  • Sweepers, sanitation workers, and gardeners
  • Home-based artisans, tailors, weavers, and handicraft workers
  • Transport workers, drivers, conductors, cart pullers, and rickshaw pullers
  • Shop workers, assistants, peons, delivery workers, and waiters
  • Washermen and mechanics

Who is Excluded from Ayushman Bharat?

Even if you fit some of the criteria above, a household will be excluded if it meets any of the following parameters:

Exclusion parameterDescriptionStatus
Motorized vehiclesOwns a motorized two-wheeler, three-wheeler, four-wheeler, or fishing boat.Excluded
Mechanized agri equipmentOwns mechanized agricultural equipment like tractors or harvesters.Excluded
KCC limitHolds a Kisan Credit Card with a credit limit exceeding Rs 50,000.Excluded
Government employmentAny member of the household is a regular, permanent government employee.Excluded
Income tax payersAny member of the family pays income tax or professional tax.Excluded
High landholdingOwns more than 5 acres of irrigated agricultural land or 7.5 acres of dry land.Excluded
Air conditioning and appliancesOwns a refrigerator, air conditioner, or landline phone.Excluded

Eligibility for Senior Citizens (70 Years and Above)

After the late-2024 expansion, all senior citizens aged 70 years and above are eligible for Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY health cover regardless of their socio-economic status.

Key highlights:

  • Senior citizens aged 70+ receive a distinct Ayushman Card.
  • They get an exclusive top-up cover of Rs 5 lakh per year shared among the seniors in the family.
  • Seniors already covered under another public health insurance scheme may choose whether to stay with it or opt into PM-JAY.

Required Documents for Ayushman Card Registration

To register or check your name in the PMJAY beneficiary list, keep the following documents ready for verification:

  • Aadhaar Card: Primary document required for identity and biometric e-KYC verification.
  • Ration Card (NFSA): Essential proof for validating household and family member count.
  • Active mobile number: Required for OTP authentication on the official portal.
  • Address proof: Useful if your current address differs from your Aadhaar record.
  • Caste certificate: Required if claiming eligibility under SC or ST rural categories.

Frequently Asked Questions

How can I check my name in the official PMJAY list?

You can check your name on the official portal by using the Am I Eligible option with your mobile number, ration card number, HHD number, or name. You can also visit a Common Service Centre or empaneled hospital helpdesk.

Is there an income limit for Ayushman Bharat eligibility?

The scheme targets low-income and vulnerable households. Rural families must match SECC deprivation criteria, while urban households must fall into approved informal worker categories. Government employees and income-tax payers are excluded.

Can I apply for an Ayushman Card if I own a motorcycle?

Motorized vehicle ownership is generally treated as an exclusion signal under PM-JAY eligibility rules, so many households with a motorcycle will not qualify.

Is the PMJAY cover per person or per family?

The health cover is a family-floater benefit. The annual amount can be used by one member or shared across family members during the year.

MeraLabh.

MeraLabh is a public-facing information portal that simplifies Indian government scheme rules to help everyday citizens understand their benefits.

14555
